greeting.text: Halt. State your business in Whiterun... Hmph. Traveler, are you? Fair enough. What do you need, citizen?

options.text: What can you tell me about the city?
What do you know about Bleakfalls Barrow?
[Speech] Any work for a capable sword-arm?

city_info.text: Whiterun's a fine city — one of the last truly peaceful holds in Skyrim, if you ask me. The market's open most mornings. Belethor runs the general goods shop just past the main gate — sells a bit of everything. The Bannered Mare is the inn, good place to rest and hear news. And Dragonsreach up the hill is the Jarl's palace. Don't cause trouble there.

options.text: Any dangers I should know about?

road_dangers.text: Safe? Ha. The roads haven't been safe since... well, ever. Bandits on the western road, wolves in the wilderness. Word is a group of them have set up in Bleakfalls Barrow — ancient Nordic ruin up in the mountains west of here. We've had reports, but it's outside city jurisdiction. If you're heading that way, go armed and go careful.

options.text: I'll keep my eyes open. Thanks.

bleakfalls_info.text: Bleakfalls Barrow. Ancient Nord burial site, older than Whiterun itself. Full of draugr — the walking dead — and now bandits too, which is a charming combination. The road southwest from the city gate takes you up toward the mountains. There are traps inside; the old Nords were fond of those. I'd recommend a full pack of healing potions before you even think about going in.

guard_respect.text: Ha. Either you're very capable or very foolish. In my experience, it's usually the latter — but sometimes the gods smile on the bold. Watch yourself in there. And if you make it back, drinks are on you at the Bannered Mare.

bounty_info.text: Sword-arm, eh? Well... officially I'm not supposed to say, but there have been reports of a stolen artifact — some kind of golden claw — taken by the bandits in Bleakfalls. If someone were to recover it and return it to the inn, I imagine the reward would be generous. Not that the city can officially offer anything. You understand.

goodbye.text: Stay out of trouble, citizen. And watch your back on the roads.
